intro: Fyber and Teads overlap on scores and pricing posture, so the decision comes down to which problem each was actually built for. Fyber is mobile ad mediation and in-app bidding platform (Digital Turbine). Teads is outstream video ad marketplace for premium publishers. Pick Fyber when the job is existing fyber customers and publishers already using digital turbine's dt exchange and you accept high lock-in; pick Teads when it is content publishers monetizing mobile web video with brand-safe premium demand and you accept high lock-in. The data we have shows Fyber at opaque pricing and product information with average developer experience, and Teads at opaque pricing and product information with average developer experience. The honest trade-off: neither is universal — Fyber is a poor fit for new publishers evaluating mediation in 2026, max or levelplay are better bets, and Teads is a poor fit for mobile game developers seeking performance-driven rewarded or interstitial fill. Match the pricing model and lock-in level to how your team actually works, not the feature list.
quick_take: Fyber is for mobile ad mediation and in-app bidding platform; Teads is for outstream video ad marketplace; decide based on whether you can accept sales-call pricing.
verdict_a: Choose Fyber if you can absorb a custom-quote sales cycle and the work maps to mobile ad mediation and in-app bidding platform (Digital Turbine).
verdict_b: Choose Teads if deep integration is an acceptable price for the depth it buys and the work lines up with outstream video ad marketplace for premium publishers.

faq.a: Neither Fyber nor Teads publishes entry pricing that lets us compare directly — Fyber uses a custom model and Teads uses a custom model. Get quotes from both before assuming one is cheaper.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Fyber to Teads?
faq.a: Moving from Fyber to Teads is feasible, but Teads is rated high lock-in — expect your integrations and workflows to need rework to match its model. Budget for a parallel-run period rather than a hard cutover.

Both Fyber and Teads rate the same on developer experience (3/5). The decision on DX then comes down to taste — which CLI, UI, or workflow matches your team's habits. A short side-by-side trial is the quickest way to tell.

faq.q: Is Teads a good alternative to Fyber?
faq.a: Yes — Teads is a reasonable alternative to Fyber for existing fyber customers and publishers already using digital turbine's dt exchange.. The practical differences are custom-vs-custom billing and high-vs-high lock-in. If those fit your constraints better, treat Teads as a credible swap.
